messages.content: You are a helpful AI assistant that specializes in Azure services and cloud computing.

Azure AI Foundry is Microsoft's comprehensive platform for building, customizing, and deploying AI applications. It provides tools for fine-tuning models, creating AI agents, and managing the entire AI development lifecycle from experimentation to production deployment.
You are a helpful AI assistant that specializes in Azure services and cloud computing.
How do I deploy a fine-tuned model in Azure OpenAI?

To deploy a fine-tuned model in Azure OpenAI: 1) Complete the fine-tuning job and get your custom model ID, 2) Navigate to Azure OpenAI Studio, 3) Go to Deployments section, 4) Click 'Create new deployment', 5) Select your fine-tuned model, 6) Configure deployment settings like name and capacity, 7) Deploy and test the model endpoint.
You are a helpful AI assistant that specializes in Azure services and cloud computing.
What are the benefits of using AI agents?

AI agents offer several benefits: 1) Automated task execution and workflow orchestration, 2) 24/7 availability for handling requests, 3) Consistent responses and adherence to guidelines, 4) Integration with multiple tools and APIs, 5) Scalability to handle varying workloads, 6) Reduced manual intervention for routine tasks, 7) Customizable behavior through fine-tuning and prompt engineering.
You are a helpful AI assistant that specializes in Azure services and cloud computing.
How do I monitor Azure OpenAI usage?

To monitor Azure OpenAI usage: 1) Use Azure Monitor for metrics and logs, 2) Check the Azure OpenAI Studio for usage dashboard, 3) Set up diagnostic settings to collect detailed logs, 4) Configure alerts for quota limits and errors, 5) Use Azure Cost Management for billing analysis, 6) Review API metrics like request rates and token consumption, 7) Implement application-level logging for detailed tracking.
You are a helpful AI assistant that specializes in Azure services and cloud computing.
What is the difference between fine-tuning and prompt engineering?

Fine-tuning vs Prompt Engineering: Fine-tuning involves training the model on custom data to modify its weights permanently, creating a new specialized model version. It's ideal for domain-specific language, consistent formatting, or when you have substantial training data. Prompt engineering involves crafting effective prompts and system messages to guide the base model's behavior without changing the model itself. It's faster, more cost-effective, and suitable for most use cases where good prompting can achieve desired results.
